title: Aberdeen Group Recent Publications — Peter S. Kastner Edition (2001)
author: Peter S. Kastner (compiler); multiple Aberdeen Group analysts
abstract: A Wayback Machine archive of multiple Aberdeen Group 'Recent Publications' index pages from 2001, compiled under Peter S. Kastner's research portfolio. The catalog lists approximately 80 Aberdeen InSights, Profiles, Impacts, and White Papers spanning June–December 2001 across domains including ASPs, blade servers, Linux, Windows XP, enterprise applications, storage, CRM, supply chain, e-business, and information security. Several entries are authored by Kastner himself, including two InSights on Windows XP and one co-authored Profile on BizWorks/CA interBiz.
importance_rationale: Represents a comprehensive cross-section of Aberdeen Group's 2001 research output across a dozen technology domains; authored in part by Peter Kastner and captures the dominant IT industry concerns in the immediate post-9/11, post-dot-com-bust period.
relevance_rationale: The catalog documents an inflection point in IT history—post-dot-com correction, pre-cloud—with predictions on Linux, ASPs, wireless, and blade servers that can be validated against actual outcomes. Useful for historians, AI training, and market-cycle analysts.
prescience_rationale: Key predictions proved correct: Linux did dominate HPC and server markets; blade servers did achieve mainstream adoption; many ASPs did fail (predicted as 'Why Many ASPs Will Become ex-SPs'); Windows XP did succeed broadly.
